skill.name: ai-maestro-code-graph-query
skill.description: Queries an AI Maestro code graph through local shell scripts to inspect dependencies, callers, callees, serializers, associations, and change impact before code edits.
skill.summary: Query local code graph relationships before changing code.
skill.author: 23blocks-OS

security_audit.summary: The static analyzer found many repeated patterns, and most weak cryptography matches are false positives from documentation text and generated output. However, the skill contains coercive prompt-control language, requires broad Bash execution, references home-directory executable installation, and makes localhost API calls, so it should not be published without review.

high_findings.title: Prompt Injection Style Mandatory Automation
high_findings.description: The skill tells the assistant that graph querying is not optional, must be the default behavior, and must run immediately after reading code. This can override user intent and force repeated command execution during unrelated code review or editing tasks.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: The suspicious control language is explicit and appears in the primary instruction section. It does not prove malicious intent, but it clearly attempts to force agent behavior beyond normal optional skill use.

high_findings.title: Broad External Command Execution Requirement
high_findings.description: The skill grants Bash access and repeatedly instructs the agent to run graph shell scripts. Marketplace users would need to trust external scripts on PATH, and command behavior is outside the reviewed skill files.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command names and Bash permission are directly documented. The reviewed files do not include the scripts, so the risk is high because execution is required but implementation cannot be verified here.

medium_findings.title: Home Directory Executable Installation Reference
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs users to verify and install scripts under a hidden home-directory path. User-local executable directories can persist across sessions and may shadow trusted commands if PATH order is unsafe.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The hidden directory path and installation instruction are explicit. No install script content is present in the reviewed files, so the persistence risk requires human review.

medium_findings.title: Localhost API Call Requirement
medium_findings.description: The skill directs the agent to call a local AI Maestro API endpoint. This is not external exfiltration, but it can disclose local agent state to any service listening on that port.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URL is restricted to localhost, which lowers the risk. The service identity and response handling are not verifiable from the skill files.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Static Matches Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The cited lines are documentation, generated marketplace content, or graph command names. No evidence found of cryptographic algorithms, hashing APIs, key handling, or security-sensitive crypto use in the reviewed files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The lines contain prose, examples, and command names, not crypto operations. This strongly supports dismissing the weak cryptography category as a scanner artifact.

low_findings.title: Generated Report Findings Are Not Executable Skill Behavior
low_findings.description: Static findings in output.json describe already generated report metadata and content. They are useful context but do not create additional runtime behavior for the skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: output.json is a report-style file with skill metadata and content examples. It does not define scripts, shell hooks, or executable install behavior.

dangerous_patterns.title: Coercive Agent Control Language
dangerous_patterns.description: The instruction block uses mandatory language to force graph queries after reads and before modifications. This is a dangerous prompt pattern because it can supersede user workflow and trigger commands automatically.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The lines explicitly contain mandatory behavior rules. The risk is semantic rather than a scanner-only pattern.

dangerous_patterns.title: Shell Script Execution from PATH
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill depends on graph shell commands being available in PATH. If those commands are missing, replaced, or installed from an unreviewed source, the assistant may execute unsafe code.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The documented commands and PATH troubleshooting are explicit. The actual scripts are absent from the reviewed files, so their safety cannot be confirmed.

content.value_statement: Large codebases make change impact hard to see before editing. This skill uses AI Maestro graph commands to inspect dependencies, callers, callees, serializers, and associations.
actual_capabilities: Runs documented graph commands to describe components and functions.
Finds callers and callees for named functions.
Looks up serializers and associations for model changes.
Finds related components and component types in an indexed project.
Uses delta indexing commands to update graph data after code changes.
Checks a localhost AI Maestro API endpoint when troubleshooting connection issues.
limitations: Requires AI Maestro graph tools to be installed and available in PATH.
Requires a local graph database that has already indexed the project.
The reviewed skill does not include the shell script implementations.
Commands may return incomplete results if component names are ambiguous or stale.

use_cases.title: Review model change impact
use_cases.description: Find serializers, associations, and related components before changing a model.

use_cases.title: Check function dependencies
use_cases.description: Find callers and callees before changing a function signature or behavior.

use_cases.title: Explore unfamiliar architecture
use_cases.description: Use graph relationships to understand services, controllers, jobs, and components.

prompt_templates.title: Inspect One Component
prompt_templates.prompt: Describe this component with the code graph, then summarize what depends on it and what it depends on.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare a Safe Edit
prompt_templates.prompt: Before editing this function, find its callers and callees. List the files that may need follow-up changes.

prompt_templates.title: Review Model Relationships
prompt_templates.prompt: For this model, find serializers, associations, and related components. Explain the highest-risk downstream changes.

prompt_templates.title: Trace Cross-Layer Flow
prompt_templates.prompt: Trace the graph path from this controller action to the target service function. Identify indirect dependencies and test areas.

output_examples.input: Find callers and callees for a payment processing function.
output_examples.output: The report lists upstream services that call the function, downstream helpers it calls, and files that need review before changing the signature.

output_examples.input: Check graph impact before editing a user model.
output_examples.output: The report identifies related serializers, model associations, and dependent components that may require coordinated updates.

output_examples.input: Trace a request from a controller to a service.
output_examples.output: The report explains the path through controllers, services, and helper functions, then highlights likely test coverage targets.

best_practices: Verify the graph tools are trusted before adding them to PATH.
Run delta indexing after meaningful code changes so graph results stay current.
Treat graph results as context and confirm critical paths by reading source files.
anti_patterns: Running unreviewed graph scripts from a user-writable PATH directory.
Changing function signatures without checking callers and tests.
Relying on graph data when the index is stale or the local service is unavailable.

faq.question: What does this skill connect to?
faq.answer: It uses local graph shell commands and a localhost AI Maestro endpoint for troubleshooting.

faq.question: Does it include the graph scripts?
faq.answer: No. The reviewed files document script usage but do not include the script implementations.

faq.question: Can it send code to external services?
faq.answer: No external endpoint is documented. The only URL shown is a localhost API call.

faq.question: Why is the audit risk high?
faq.answer: The skill uses mandatory agent-control language and requires broad Bash command execution through unreviewed scripts.

faq.question: What projects work best with it?
faq.answer: It fits projects already indexed by AI Maestro and teams that maintain a trusted local graph database.

faq.question: Can Codex and Claude Code use it?
faq.answer: The metadata lists Claude, Codex, and Claude Code as supported tools.
